title.purpose: Stat-tile values — streak counts and totals. The boldest type in the widget.

body.purpose: Base size on :host; loading and error messages.

label.purpose: Stat-tile captions and legend chips, in Muted Ink.

micro.purpose: SVG axis labels — month names and weekday markers.

shadows.name: floating-tip
shadows.purpose: The only shadow in the system. Lifts the position-fixed tooltip off the content it hovers over.

motion.name: none
motion.purpose: The widget defines no CSS transitions; repaints are instant. Any future motion must convey state, stay within 150–250ms, and respect prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Stat Tile
components.description: A single metric: a bold value over a muted caption. Reflows in an auto-fit grid.
components.css: .ds-stat-tile { background: #f6f8fa; border: 1px solid #d0d7de; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 10px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-stat-tile-val { font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; color: #1f2328; } .ds-stat-tile-lbl { font-size: 11px; color: #656d76; }

components.name: Legend Chip
components.description: A pill button, one per source: color swatch, label, range total. Click toggles or isolates the layer.
components.css: .ds-legend-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 8px; border: 1px solid #d0d7de; border-radius: 16px; background: #f6f8fa; color: #1f2328; cursor: pointer; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; user-select: none; } .ds-legend-chip:hover { border-color: #8c959f; } .ds-legend-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2da44e; outline-offset: 1px; } .ds-legend-swatch { width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 2px; flex-shrink: 0; background: #2da44e; }

components.name: Legend Chip (off)
components.description: A toggled-off layer. Dimmed to 0.45; the swatch keeps its hue so identity survives.

components.name: Heatmap Cells
components.description: Left: a single-source day at intensity 0.6. Right: a two-source day split into equal color stripes — the Overlay Rule made visible.
components.html: <svg class="ds-cells" width="39" height="12" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Heatmap cells"><rect x="0" y="0" width="12" height="12" rx="2" fill="#2da44e" fill-opacity="0.6"/><g><rect x="27" y="0" width="6" height="12" fill="#2da44e" fill-opacity="0.8"/><rect x="33" y="0" width="6" height="12" fill="#fc6d26" fill-opacity="0.4"/></g></svg>

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: The one floating surface. Formatted date plus each active source's exact count.
components.css: .ds-tooltip { display: inline-block; padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 6px; background: #1f2328; color: #fff; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.25); }

narrative.overview: Every source a developer connects is a distinct colored signal, and the widget stacks them onto one 53-by-7 grid without ever flattening them into a single number. The lineage is the observability dashboard — high-contrast, engineered, comfortable in dark mode — not the marketing chart. The widget renders on sites its author will never see, so the shell is deliberately quiet and the color does the talking: flat containers with hairline borders, the host's own system font, nothing floating except the tooltip. The energy is bold but comes from the data — saturated layer colors, dense cells, sharp edges — never from gradients, glows, or motion. It must look intentional on a stranger's homepage and fit a 15 KB gzip budget with zero dependencies.
keyCharacteristics: Multi-source overlay: N active sources on a day split the cell into N equal color stripes, never merged.
Intensity rides opacity, not hue: a source keeps one color; its volume is expressed 0.2 to 0.8.
Shadow-DOM isolated, host system font, flat shell, under 15 KB.
Dark-mode-native, crisp and high-contrast; bold through data, not decoration.

rules.name: The Overlay Rule
rules.body: Sources never merge. A day with N visible active sources divides into N equal-width vertical stripes, each in its source color. Reducing a multi-source day to one blended color or one total is forbidden — the separation is the product.

rules.name: The Opacity Ramp Rule
rules.body: Intensity is expressed through opacity, never through hue shifts. Each source keeps one color; the day's volume maps to five levels (0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8) bucketed by quartile against that source's own non-zero history. Hue is identity; opacity is volume.

rules.name: The System-Font Rule
rules.body: The widget ships no fonts and loads none. It borrows whatever system-ui resolves to on the host. Adding a web font is forbidden: it breaks the load-instantly promise and the gzip budget.

rules.name: The Flat-Shell, Floating-Tip Rule
rules.body: Every resting surface — tiles, chips, cells — is flat with a hairline border. The tooltip is the only thing in the system that casts a shadow, because it is the only thing that floats. If a container has a shadow, it is wrong.

dos: Do keep the multi-source overlay intact: split a shared day into equal per-source stripes; never blend or total them away.
Do express intensity through opacity (0.2 to 0.8) on a fixed source hue.
Do carry source identity redundantly — color plus legend label plus tooltip — so two close hues still read as two sources for color-blind viewers.
Do use flat surfaces with 1px hairline borders; reserve the only shadow for the tooltip.
Do stay on the host's system-ui stack and inside the 15 KB gzip budget; ship no web fonts and no framework.
Do keep the shell quiet so the widget looks native on any host page, and let the layer color be the boldest thing on screen.
donts: Don't make it look like a corporate analytics dashboard — no KPI cards, filter bars, or enterprise chrome.
Don't let it read as a bare GitHub clone — a one-to-one copy of the green-squares calendar throws away the multi-source overlay.
Don't go loud or overdesigned — no gradients, glows, glassmorphism, or decorative motion.
Don't use gradient text or a background-clip: text fill anywhere.
Don't use a colored side-stripe border (border-left/border-right > 1px) on tiles, chips, or callouts.
Don't add a web font, a framework dependency, or anything that risks the load-instantly promise or the size budget.
